marketState: neutral / mixed - bearish near-term technicals and storage overhang vs a bullish medium-term weather catalyst
summary: Price at $2.912 is sitting almost exactly on the model's fair value ($2.919) with no meaningful mispricing at any horizon, so there is no fair-value edge to trade today. The medium-term picture is more constructive than the tape looks: the 8-16 day weather outlook is running +46.2% above the 5-year norm and above its historical max range, which should lift cooling demand and is already pulling the fair-value curve higher (from $2.919 now to $3.034 in 21 days). That bullish weather signal is reinforced by a heavily crowded managed-money net short (8th percentile of 5 years), which raises squeeze risk on any supportive news. Working against that: storage is +183 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise this week, seasonality reliability is low (63pp spread), and technicals are bearish on nearly every timeframe from 5-minute to weekly with several 50/200 death crosses. Net read: lean cautiously long for the medium-term weather/squeeze setup, but respect the bearish short-term technical and storage backdrop before it's confirmed by price action.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; trim ~half at TP1, trail the rest
longTerm.rationale: The 8-16D demand outlook is extreme (+46.2% vs 5y, above the historical max) and the fair-value curve already reflects rising demand out to 21 days ($2.919 -> $3.034); combined with managed money sitting at the 8th percentile of 5-year net-short positioning, there is real squeeze potential if the heat forecast verifies and storage builds slow. Seasonality's 69% odds of higher prices in 1 and 4 weeks add mild support, though reliability is flagged low this year.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage keeps printing bearish surprises (bigger builds than forecast), the 8-16D heat signal rolls over back toward normal, or front price breaks and holds below the fair-value band low (~$2.42-2.53).

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 0.5-1% account risk given tighter stop and low conviction
shortTerm.rationale: Near-term (1-7D) weather is close to normal (-1.1% vs 5y), giving technicals and the fresh bearish storage surprise (+17 Bcf vs forecast) the deciding vote for this horizon; trend and EMA cross (50<200) are bearish on 5m/1h/4h/1d/1w and MACD is negative on most of those, with price offering no fair-value edge (status FAIR everywhere).
shortTerm.invalidation: A close back above $3.02, RSI turning up through 50 with a bullish MACD cross, or an early pull-forward of the hot 8-16D weather signal into the 1-7D window.

keyDrivers: 8-16D weather demand +46.2% vs 5y and above the historical max range - rising cooling demand ahead
Managed money net short at the 8th percentile of 5 years - crowded short, squeeze risk
Storage +183 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish build surprise this week (+17 Bcf vs forecast)
Price is FAIR vs model now, but the fair-value curve is drifting higher over the next 3 weeks
Technicals bearish (death crosses, negative MACD) across nearly every timeframe from 5m to 1w
risks: 8-16D weather forecasts can shift quickly and the extreme heat signal may not verify
Crowded short could keep pressing the market lower instead of squeezing, extending the bearish move
Seasonality reliability is low this year (63pp 10-90% spread), weakening the seasonal tailwind as a guide
